The prices are reasonable and you get a lot better service than you can get from any online retailer.  It's worth it just to visit because you'll learn a lot about what's available; we've bought things there we didn't know existed and have been delighted with the result.  It's also been a great place for gifts during the holidays because of the diversity of products they offer.

My favorite mexican restaurant. The ingredients are so fresh everything is made to order, their home made salsa is the best.  Can't go wrong with anything on the menu. Foods not grease like you get at a lot of Mexican places

Portions are very generous, you will not leave hungry. Only open for lunch and only takes cash. And it can get busy for lunch but where downtown doesn't.

All in all great food, excellent staff and a great Price. My go to Mexican pick

This has to be the best diner setting Downtown, a complete archetypal flashback of the greasy spoon meal alternative.  I'm pretty much a regular (2x a week) now so I wanted to update my review to point out that they've made a couple changes (maybe a different food purveyor) and the food is even better now.  The bread is fresher than ever, the turkey is nice and tender, and the bacon is scrumptious.  Service is still that  \"make yourself at home\" but \"don't take yourself too seriously\" that I truly enjoy.

Walk through the door and immediately the sweet, spicy scents of flowers and greens envelops your senses. Fresh green earthy ferns, spiky AND soft cacti, terrariums filled with tiny worlds you'd love to explore in Tiny Alice form all crowd antique tables, shelves and hutches - a bounty of natural wonders there for sweet discovery. 

The walls teem with both strikingly colorful and softly demure wreaths and wall accents. One glass topped and fronted case has huge jars of the most wonderful potpourri that you can scoop to your heart's content....just imagining your bedroom, living room, your entire home smelling like a little piece of heaven. 

Then, just as you pass that glass case an open door and a secret garden beckons with wonders of green outside. Melodious chimes, gurgling water, wondrous plants that you are certain deserve special places under no less than the glass roofs of Phipps Conservatory. 

What a wonderful respite from the rush and noise of the Strip....a quiet oasis. Thanks, Roxanne. Well done.

I adore Roxanne's! There are so many beautiful arrangements and terrariums, and a great variety of live plants inside and out. You can also find lovely home decor in the form of side tables and such. On that note, I have even purchased an ethically made dress here! The employees are always a pleasure to chat with, and prices are reasonable for the quality you get. Even if you're not in the market for any of the aforementioned, stop in here and fulfill your senses.

J'adore this store!!!  So beautiful and filled with many inspiration pieces. Very much on the pricey side, but a check online shows me that their stock is not too overpriced. This is def a room in heaven. :)

Oh Andy, how I miss your little swagger and sass! I know this is a difficult one to rate, since he runs a stand within another establishment (Wholeys) but I am giving 5 glowing stars nontheless for the amazing sushi he makes (right in front of you) and his stellar personality. I have never met a person with blood flowing in their veins that didn't get a total kick out of Andy.

When I worked in the Strip, I ate here almost daily. After only two visits, he remembered exactly what I ordered and would start making it as soon as he saw me: spicy yellowtail with avocado. Or just yellowtail with avocado. Maybe some cucumber. Have I mentioned that I am obsessed with yellowtail? Ahhhh, the buttery-ness, the texture, the slight taste of the sea....oh wait, this isn't a review on a singular fish, i got a little carried away (even though Andy serves up some damn fine YT)! 

Sometimes he gets creative and mashes different flavor concepts together for you to sample as you wait. He once came up with a maki roll made of salmon, basil, cucumber and mango....brilliant!!

Wholeys is such a fantastic people watching spot anyway and it's always amusing to see random people of all ages/genders/sizes walk in and yell "ANDY"!
Andy has the best Sushi. His special sauce is what makes his sushi the best.
Seriously,best sushi in Pittsburgh. Freshest ingredients, huge portions and very reasonable pricing. And yeah, Andy gives free samples if you smile at him!

Ate here last night and it was absolutely the best meal I have had at Sushi Kim. Wife got the usual pork bulgogi and I got standar dol sut bi-bim-bap. \n\nIt was unbelievably good. It wasn't because I was really hungry. It was perfect. Every single bit of both meals was perfectly proportioned and seasoned. And the side dishes were GREAT except I really missed not having sweet potato and little omelet ban chan. I will cut out Dosani and Korean Kitchen as long as Sushi Kim keeps this up!

We are so lucky to have such an amazing theater community in Pittsburgh.  This unique theater adds so much to the mix!  It's a fantastic theater because they focus on plays, usually with one musical in the mix. There's new works here, like the original show The Chief, about what else, but Pittsburgh Steelers founding owner Art Rooney.  They bring in the classics like A Midsummer Night's Dream and The Important of Being Earnest.  They also have newer works right off Broadway, like The Rabbit Hole and Superior Donuts.  Plus, they bring in Second City every year, which is big in my book because they've spawned a couple SNL cast members!! 

I absolutely LOVE the theater!  I'm a big fan of a theater in the round.  That means there's seating on 3 sides of the stage.  They've also been able to do some really cool things with the stage/set, like making an actual pool for the show Metamorphoses.  My favorite all time show was The Secret Letters of Jackie and Marilyn, which was a world premiere.  The show was about a made up relationship between the two women where they wrote letters to each other.  It was a VERY cool concept! 

They also do a lot of great events like the Mix at 6 for young professionals before a show.  Plus, they understand that most of us under 26 (and of course sometimes above) are broke ho's, they offer $15 tickets!  The great thing is, there's really not a bad seat in the house! 

Again, we have a fantastic theater district, so TAKE ADVANTAGE and go here!  Don't make me tell you twice!!

Totally underrated by the Yelpers!

I freakin' LOVE Redfin Blues.  I'm kind of glad it had a low rating previously to keep y'all away while I was living in Pittsburgh!  The only reason I'm even reviewing it now is because I have moved away and I'm not at risk for losing my table to a Yelp reader.  Also, I feel guilty at not letting people in on this gem.

To be clear, I LOVE Redfin Blues for a very SPECIFIC purpose: sitting outside on the patio by the water on a Saturday or Sunday afternoon drinking the bar out of Red Stripe and white wine and eating peel and eat shrimp by the 2 pound bowl with B, Ems, Boyfriend, and whoever else is so lucky.  It is a hidden gorgeous Pittsburgh oasis under the 31st bridge near Washington's Landing (note: the Yelp map is wrong) where you don't feel at all like you're in Pittsburgh, but instead on vacation.

Disclaimer: I have heard that at night it is full of strange frat boys (from no actual fraternity), Cougars, tramps, creatures of the night  -- or undesirables.  I don't go at night so my credibility wanes here a little.

I basically only ever eat the peel and eat shrimp.  It's just cooked shrimp - sometimes it's big, sometimes it's little.  But it's $17 for TWO pounds which isn't too overpriced especially since it can feed a tableful of friends.  Ask for extra old bay if you like it spicy and salty - yum! I've also had the fried cod sandwich - the fish is crisp and flaky and darn good!  I always take it off the bread to keep it as crispy as possible but those who know me know I have an obsession with fried crispy-ness.  The breading isn't too too heavy so that is a plus too.  I've also had the crab cakes which are meh  but fine for Pittsburgh standards and I would definitely steer clear of the crab legs.  

Ok, so really you should go to this place - if you like peel and eat shrimp, cold drinks by the river, and occasionally a keyboardist who plays and sings in the background pretty quietly.  Some of my favorite Pittsburgh moments happened here and that's saying a LOT.  Redfin Blues makes me miss you Pittsburgh!

This is SUCH a gorgeous boutique -- shabby chic furnishings galore! If you're in the market for truly unique, special furniture and live anywhere near Pittsburgh, Hot Haute Hot is the place you're looking for. 

I came here with my friend who had long been on a quest to find a new dining room table. The options here were so gorgeous I was immediately struck with envy. There are curio cabinets, amazing reclaimed ceiling tile mirrors, plush chairs, fantastic lamps and a bevy of side tables, wall hangings and of course, dining room tables. 

The service is impeccable -- sweet, helpful women who know every item in their shop and are just as excited to help you shop as you are to find perfect item. The taste level in this store is impressive, as well. 

As a bonus, they have a few displays of fun, affordable jewelry ranging from delicate to super funky. Take your time to pick through this shop and enjoy yourself while doing it -- I certainly did!

Just as I was about to give up on finding a downtown hair salon, I found Denham & Company and Dan!  Quite a nice cut and those yummy Aveda products, to boot.  

Comfortably eclectic is how I'd describe the shop in Market Square just to the right of NOLA and up a steep flight of stairs.  Antiques on beautiful hardwood floors interspersed with contemporary salon chairs make for warm and friendly surroundings, which is just exactly what I found in the staff, too...warm and friendly AND skilled.

Thanks Dan AND Denham & Company!  You've restored my faith in downtown hair salons!

Sheer ecstatic bliss is what this place should be called! wonderful talented friendly hairdressers!  My friend and I went there for the first time today, let them cut our follicle fillers as they saw fit, and have never been more satisfied in our short little lives.\n\nIf you want bumpin' style and sexy looks, HIT IT UP!!!!

I've tried many different salons throughout the city since moving here 6 years ago, and once I found Tim at Denham & Co, I haven't gone anywhere else! My hair is crazy thick and I always have troubles coloring it but Tim always gets the perfect shade and cuts it in the best way. I get so many compliments on the color anywhere I go. The price is definitely worth it, plus you'll have a great time while you're there - staff is super friendly and talkative. If you're looking for stylish look, need help with color, suggestions for a overall new look - go here! 2 of my best friends already have gone here after I did.
Dan is awesome!
Julie does a fantastic job. Been going to her for years. The attention to detail is tremendous and I come away with a haircut I can have confidence in.

I'm not sure why this place isn't getting better reviews! I've been here twice over the last couple months (including yesterday)  and have been very happy each time I've gone.

Caffe Amante is located on the second floor (food court) level of Fifth Avenue Place, the home of HighMark Blue Cross Blue Shield.

Amongst most of the mediocre chain food that's served on the same floor, it stands out as basically the only(or one of a few) sit down places available.  

Both times the service was right on point.  Quick, efficient, despite being busy, friendly, wait-until-I-finish-my-sentence-sensitive, good recommendations on food.  Corrected themselves when they were wrong in what they told me previously and so on.

The two dishes I've had are:

Special Salmon and jumbo lump crab meat.  It was expertly cooked, great flavors, good portion size, and overall just a winning dish.

Di Mare Amante which is a dish with shrimp, scallops, in a white clam sauce with capellini noodles.  Their menu spells it with two p's: cappellini.

Both were great and I'd have them again.

You know, part of my review and rating is based on exactly what this place is: a more upscale place for the mostly business clientele bringing their customers out for a conveniently located lunch. In a restaurant that is more or less in a food court. Being surrounded chinese joints, mexican burrito places, and the philly cheese steak place.

While I think it would be a solid bet for dinner as well --- that's not how I see this place positioned. I wouldn't take a date here Friday or Saturday night. There are more exciting places offering more adventures fare.

This is not your $8 lunch.... most things are priced $12-$16 which includes bread. Their prices well justified by the quality of the food.

For lunch, I'd go back to this place in a heartbeat.

Located downtown, call Dr. Greg and he can get you in that day, sometimes within the hour.  He's a cool guy, very friendly, and not a new-age, hippy, sales-y doctor like some of the other chiropractors that I've been to.  Accepts insurance & FSA...if you work downtown, I recommend him.

Everyone at Salon Nuvo is a professional.  Everyone is friendly and it's a really great salon.  Chrissy has done my hair for almost 15 years (she moved to Salon Nuvo about 2 years ago) and I trust no one else to perm or color my hair.  Chrissy understands chemicals and how to maintain healthy hair.  She's a dedicated professional and has always been flexible - she let's me come in late to accommodate my work/school schedule.  And everyone I've ever referred to her has been a happy customer :-)  I'm pretty brutal on my hair (lots of heat and don't deep condition as often as I should, in addition to a fair amount of every day stresses)....If Chrissy didn't maintain and take care of my hair, I'd probably be bald by now!  She keeps it trimmed, healthy, and growing!!!  And she's super sweet....it's a delight to sit in her chair :-)

I have been going to Salon Nuvo for about two years now. Autumn does my hair and she is amazing. I have very fine, thinning hair and the cut is always flattering. And Autumn seems quite supportive of my desire to not color my very grey hair, which is something I have not had in other salons ... there has always been a push to do some sort of coloring treatment.

I came here for lunch recently and all was excellent. I come here about 4 times a year. When I do go I prepare myself by not eating red meat several days prior to going. On the day of I make sure to eat a normal but smaller breakfast. No bagels as they occupy too much space. I arrive a bit later, I am a bit hungrier and the staff that remains are long time regulars who know me but are also there to earn a living and make my visit worthwhile. I always order a filet, medium with mushrooms. I don't drink when go, the beef is too good to have my senses misdirected. The plate and filet arrive in minutes, the plate is sizzling at 500 degrees, if by chance there is too much red in the meat I cut a piece and sizzle it right there on the plate. I recently tried the salmon on a recommendation from the owner and was wowed how good it was. Panko crumbs and lemon, great stuff. \nI always feel they go the extra mile for me but the food and service are always top notch i can assume this is the standard.

Always amazing! My Husband and I frequent Ruth's Chris for almost every special-occasion: birthdays, anniversaries, when we need a great meal; and without fail we leave euphoric. 

After almost each bite, we close our eyes to shut-out the sense of sight to focus on the taste and aroma of the garlicky, creaminess of the Remoulade on our salads; the richness of the Pesto on our Sea Scallops; the buttery-goodness of our dry-aged steak; and the salty crunch of our French Fries, which Kathy suggested we dip into Honey-mustard for a fantastic salty-sweet combination.

Friday is a good reason to go... Reservations are calling!
I loved my everything about my experience at Ruth Chris.  It was a bit expensive, but the service was outstanding and the food was delicious.  \nI would definitely go again.
No matter which location I go to, I am never disappointed with Ruth's Chris.  I would live here if I could...  Feeling hungry?  Try the Porterhouse...AWESOME!

Best Steaks in Pittsburgh!!! I come here a lot for special occasions.  It is a pricy place but it is worth every penny. I had a New York Strip and it was really tender and juicy.  Definitely one of my favorite restaurants.  The service is excellent also.

Always top notch service and food at Ruth's Chris Pittsburgh. They have some summery specials during these cold winter months to bring you out of the post-holiday funk, which is very refreshing to see. Stop in for lunch for great deals on amazing food. Their lunch cuts of beef, chicken and fish include 2 sides and a salad for an extremely reasonable price. I got the lunch petite filet, veggies, mashed potatoes and appetizer salad for 28 bucks! Not bad for a steakhouse. Our server was incredible, as usual, and was extremely attentive of my gluten-free needs. The servers are extremely knowledgeable of the menu and will accommodate you no matter what. The decor is a bit dated and is due for a remodeling, but perhaps that will be on the list of things to do for the chain since they are celebrating their 25th anniversary this year!

I read an article recently in the Post-Gazette that says to wring the most wear out of what they own, people are visiting auto mechanics, cobblers, tailors and computer repair experts as they haven't in years.  I believe it.  Business at Ullrich's has been good recently, especially since they moved into their present location.  Ullrich's has been around in one form or another for over 80 years and a couple of years ago they moved to their present location.

Ullrich's offers a full line of services including shoe and purse repair, desalt and waterproofing, and jacket and zipper repair.  Can't make it into their store?  You can even send your shoes to be repaired via mail!  No, really!  Check it out!    http://www.ullrichshoerepair.com/mailorder.htm

Anyway, my husband Rick asked me to drop off his shoes to get them resoled here.  I just picked them up on lunch a few minutes ago and they look practically new.  He had me drop off his leather jacket to have a tear repaired last year and they did a fantastic job.  I've had one of my purses repaired here too.  We've always been satisfied with their work.  

It's so good to see people renovate and repair rather than throw out.  Rick and I have been doing that for years.

Hey, they linked to my review on their website!   Just click on News & Links and then on Customer Review http://www.ullrichshoerepair.com/newslinks.htm  Like Steve Martin in \"The Jerk\" when he finds his name in the phone book, I guess I'm somebody now!  \n\nAll kidding aside, Ullrich's came in handy last week.  I broke a heel and they did a great job repairing it.  They've resoled Rick's shoes, repaired a tear in his leather jacket, and repaired one of my purses.  We've always been happy with their work.

Miracle worker. Genius.  The guy here is amazing. This may be the best place on earth. I only recently discovered the fabulousness of having your shoes repaired instead of throwing them out. I walked in there with heels on that were so beat I was walking on metal rods (don't judge). He fixed them in about 10 minutes and they looked brand new.  

Yesterday I took my most favorite pair of boots, a unique pair of Steve Madden cognac leathers with rawhide ties up the backs, in with the hopes they could be salvaged.  The boot heals were slowly breaking down. Not only did he completely replace the heels to look like the day I bought them, but he shined them all up all pretty and suggested a cream to put on them to make them last forever.  He said they'd last 40 years. I said 'you, me and these boots are gonna be lifelong friends'. 

Best cobbler ever!!!

So I don't ever go Downtown because well Downtown is kind of icky. Eh, whatever I guess that's really besides the point. Anywho, After getting my eyebrows done at Princess Nails from Michelle (EYEBROW FAIRY!) I needed to cut a new set of keys because apparently holding onto keys is lost on me. I doubt my landlord wants to be woken up at 2:00am to let me in again either. After calling 7349651474 locksmiths that wouldn't duplicate keys because they're stooopid someone from the Office Depot sent me to Ulrichs Shoe Repair. Yes, Shoe Repair. Fix your shoe, make a key. Upon entering Ulrichs (I think it was the owner) was super friendly and I'll give him credit for being somewhat funny. Gave him my keys and in literally 30 seconds flat both keys were made AND they both worked. Usually going to somewhere like Home Depot you have to go back like 3 times for the key to work properly. Not in this case. Overall customer service amazing. Quality of service amazing. Plus super cheap too! $2 bucks a key. Um that's a deal if I ever saw one.

I've been to Ullrich's twice and both times received excellent service.

 My first time at Ullrich's was to have the end caps of my heels replaced (for a second time). Initially I went to another repair store in Sq Hill and had them replaced -- but the replacement heels were totally wore out again within a couple months.  Went to Ullrich's to have them redone and after 6 months of hard walking, the heels are still wearing great.  

I won't go any where else.

I am really careful with how my shoes are handled. Every time I buy a new pair of shoes, I immediately have sole protection covered. This was actually my first time doing anything like this in the states, and I really wanted to go to a place that does a perfect job. \n\nAt the shop I met Rex, whom he was very kind and friendly. Still, I was still skeptical about this shop so before I departed after giving my new pair of shoes I asked a one last final request to be EXTRA careful with my shoes. And guess what? He did an amazing job and I am really happy how he got it to be perfectly thin as I wanted.

I can't find a complaint about this place.  Drinks are reasonable.  Only ever seems to be a $1 cover.  Couches for lounging.  Pool table.  Room for dancing if you want to (no one hardly seems to).\n\nStaff is super friendly.  During Pride it is a place you can stop into to get a drink cheaper than the beer tents outside.

The owner and her parents are delightful, helpful and kind. I often stop in here for a little treat or blast from the past. I grew up in England and love that I can buy chocolates that I loved as a child. If you need a recommendation or a gift, they are always willing to help you choose something or put together a little gift basket within your price range.

I don't think I've ever seen this great a variety of chocolate assembled in one place in my life! I think Augustus Gloop might have a heart attack and die in here, clutching a chocolate bar in his hand. 

They have chocolate from all over the world in weird and exotic flavors to normal ones too. My two nephews were allowed to pick anything they wanted, and so were my husband and I. We didn't buy much, but what we did buy, between us all, cost my sister about $100! On chocolate!!

I think it's a great place to stop in if you have a choco hankering, or are in the market to try something new.

Soft, made to order bagels. Great low-fat smoothies. Staff is always nice and patient with my gazillion order changes. There's so many great choices! They also have texting sign-ups and I get free coffee and bagels with my orders all the time.

We just happened to wander in after an afternoon of exploring Pittsburgh.  What a gem!  What a lucky place to stumble in!

Klavon's was a drugstore/soda fountain in the early 1900s.  All the original counters, glass cases filled with old bottles, stools, and mixers are still there.  We had the turtle sundae with amaretto whipped cream, which was amazing.  I can't wait to come back and try their phosphates and egg creams!  It was like stepping into a time machine. You can tell this place is beloved by locals; several elderly couples, families with children, and single people with dogs on leash came by for cones, sundaes, and floats on the beautiful sunny day.  The grandson of the original owners was chatting with customers, and the place had a really special vibe.

You almost never see places like this anymore - if you're in Pittsburgh, don't miss it!

When I die I hope I come back as an ice cream bowl at Klavon's so I can forever spend my days covered in mint chocolate chip and watching people smile. I don't know...maybe not, but I do love mint chocolate chip and old skool ice cream parlors and Klavon's does both really well. 

This is a list of things at Klavon's that make me feel like vintage stuff isn't only for grandparents and ice cream isn't just for kids: Amaretto flavored whipped cream, swivel stools with red tops, high backed wooden booths, a man behind the counter with a pure white handlebar mustache, a hammered tin ceiling, and large pecan balls.

You can go with chocolate or vanilla, but when I get the chance to have a pecan ball, I'm no idiot, I take it. A pecan ball, my friend, is ice cream rolled in pecans, covered in hot fudge or caramel and then topped with whipped cream. That's all tasty, nutty, fudgy goodness, but if you wake up feeling like painting the town gold and you want something that tastes like it was made for Cleopatra by Mark Antony's own hands, get a scoop of coffee, a scoop of pecan and top it with Amaretto whipped cream: I'm hoping to get this added to the menu under the name "Magic Magic Explosion." 

They have a picture of Joe Biden posing with the owners; it's taped to the mirror behind the bar and quite adorable. "He was very nice and left $20 for the next little girl in line to buy her ice cream with," the owner told me.  Also, $20 worth of ice cream?! Two scoops only runs five bucks. Joe Biden--excuse me, Mr. Biden--you are a true American hero and I, for one, am happy someone finally realized the importance of children having tons and tons and tons of ice cream. 

Good show, sir, good show.

Worth a visit from across the country. Klavon's recalls about 100 years ago with its true ice cream soda fountain days. It's been in the same family since around the strike of 1900 when it was a drug store and turned into this sometime in the last century. The styling seems to be a better and more true throwback than even Pittsburgh itself. Who would not like this has no soul.\n\nI only sampled the hot chocolate and two types of whipped cream, but I give it 5-stars anyways. I feel like this is an intelligent guess. The whipped cream was my favorite of all time, the amaretto whip didn't even strike me as amaretto, it swooned me lightly.

There are five reasons why you might want to go to Klavon's.
1. You're in the Strip District anyway.
2. You're interested in Pittsburgh history.
3. You've never experienced an old-time soda fountain before.
4. You're looking for some hard-to-find penny candy.
5. You want some damn good ice cream.

I went for all five reasons, and I wasn't disappointed. Wait. That doesn't express the depths of my enthusiasm for Klavon's. KLAVON'S RULES!!!!!!!!! There, that's better.

Let's take it in order. You have lots of dining options in the Strip. Now, Klavon's is a few blocks away from most of the popular places (we were at Salem's, which is also great, but that's another review), but it's easy to walk to and easy to find with all the neon signs.

When you walk in to Klavon's, you're taken back in time. Choose a little booth or table, and gaze at the decor. Maybe you'll see an old liniment bottle from back when Klavon's was a drugstore. Maybe you'll notice the high-water mark from the 1936 flood (close to the ceiling!). Maybe you'll see the old penny scale or the phone booths. This is a place where history is celebrated.

The menu isn't extensive, but you still have lots of choices. Why not try an old-time phosphate, malted, or egg cream? It was good enough for your great-grandpa; it's good enough for you. I treated myself to a root beer float. It was the best I ever had. The reason? Both the root beer and the ice cream were made locally. On the way out, why not browse the penny candy racks? For a quarter, you can try a bunch of hard-to-find treats.

I'd be remiss if I didn't mention that Mr. Klavon, the grandson (I think) of the original owner, still runs the place. A glipse of his epic mustache alone is worth the visit, but he's always willing to give you a tour. Friendly people in friendly Pittsburgh. I can't wait to go back!
Easy 5. Authentic old school ice cream parlor with PSU ice cream ( peachy paterno etc). The ice cream soda was an outstanding ride in the wayback machine.

Just found this gem.  This is a great place for every ice cream lover.  Great place to hang out with friends or to go on a date.\n\nI recommend the sundaes at this place.  This is pure 50's and 60's style that isn't forced.  Staff are very friendly and patient.
Retta is knowledgeable, intuitive and strong.  She gives a great massage.

I used to go to Sammys all the time for a corned beef sandwich whenever i came in town....until my mom took me to Smallman Street Deli! Best corned beef on rye...be sure to get it grilled!!! My sandwich...lean corned beef on marble rye, swiss cheese, grilled with lettuce, tomato, onion and spicy brown mustard!! Love this place!
Great theater !!! Absolutely beautiful .. Live the seating ... Seen Book of Mormon here !!! Loved it
I like coming here. Saw the Lion King. Bathrooms get crowded so you better hurry.  I'll be back.

Giving my local businesses some loving. I frequent here & will never gives money elsewhere. There is a touch less car wash right down the street for the same prices who cleans half what this place does. I personally prefer an actual hand wash anyday. If you do come here, those these guys a buck or 2 in the tip. I've never had a guy here that didn't make sure every little mark or wet spot was off the car before I drove off the lot.

1.0: I loved the Mediterranean Bistro, their Greek salad is exceptional with the best feta cheese and fresh veggies, and the server's knowledge of the menu was surprising, considering she wasn't a huge foodie. The ambiance is cozy and intimate, not crowded at all despite being downtown, and the music added a pleasant touch, great place for a pleasant dinner with a friend or family. The service was attentive but not overbearing, what more could you ask for in a dining experience?

"Highly recommended the vegan sushi place down the street, the service was attentive and knowledgeable staff. Ordered the spicy tuna roll and the pad thai was a nice touch, the portion sizes are generous and the spicy sauce was just right. A bit pricey, but totally worth it for such a unique dining experience."

"I've been to several restaurants in the area, but my experience at Bistro Bliss has been subpar so far. The atmosphere is cozy, and the decor is modern, but the food quality has been quite inconsistent. I've ordered a few dishes that were overcooked, and the service has been slow. My server seemed distracted and forgot my drink order when I asked for a refill. For the prices they're charging, I expected better quality. Maybe they're still working out some kinks, but I'd caution potential diners to be patient."

"The highly praised café has seriously elevated its downtown location, offering a sophisticated atmosphere and remarkable service, thanks to the newly renovated interior that exudes modern elegance; however, I can attest that the most significant accomplishment lies in the excellent food quality, particularly their signature pastry selection that perfectly balances traditional flavors with a touch of creative flair."

I've had the pleasure of dining at this quaint little Italian restaurant on my last trip to Little Italy. The pasta dishes were exquisite, featuring delicate flavors and an array of vibrant colors that showcased the chef's attention to detail. What truly set the dish apart was the exceptional service; our server was attentive and knowledgeable, providing seamless recommendations based on the specials of the evening. What made this experience truly memorable was the lively atmosphere, replete with enchanting background music and soft, warm lighting, creating an intimate setting reminiscent of a classic supper club. Overall, this restaurant has secured a spot in my list of go-to destinations for culinary excellence.

I stopped at Café Bistro Bliss downtown, and oh man, you won't regret it! The ambiance was intimate and cozy, with dim lighting and plush sofas that made you feel like you're in for a treat. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable, recommending the burrata appetizer which we enthusiastically indulged in – creamy, rich, and paired perfectly with the house red wine. For my main course, I opted for the grilled salmon, which was seared to perfection and bursting with flavor. The portion size was generous and filling, and let us save some for a late evening snack. Overall, I highly recommend Café Bistro Bliss to anyone looking for a romantic evening out or just a superb meal.

"The best thing about Joe's Diner is the friendly service - the waitstaff went above and beyond to ensure we had a great experience, from the complimentary appetizers to the expertly cooked burgers. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, perfect for a date night or a quiet evening out with friends. Definitely worth the hype - can't wait to come back and try more of the menu items!"

"I had the most incredible dining experience at Bistro Bliss last night, the menu was diverse and the portions were generously sized. Our server, Rachel, was incredibly attentive and knowledgeable, helped us navigate the extensive wine list and made excellent recommendations. The food was truly exceptional, with the burgers being some of the best I've ever had. The atmosphere was lively and upscale, perfect for a special occasion or a night out with friends. Although the prices were a bit steep, it was well worth it, can't wait to come back and try more of their seasonal specials."

"As I've had the pleasure of visiting the Cozy Bistro three times now, I must say that it's been an absolute gem on every occasion. The service has been top-notch each time, with friendly and attentive wait staff that made every table feel like their own. The food, oh the food - we've had the pasta dishes, the burgers, and even took a chance on the pan-seared salmon, and it was cooked to perfection every time. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and soft music that creates the perfect ambiance for a romantic evening. The prices are very reasonable considering the quality of the food and the outstanding service. Will definitely be back to try more of their dishes and explore the full menu. 5/5 stars, every time!"

"I'm extremely impressed with the new menu items at Bistro Bliss - the truffle pizza was out of this world and the service was top-notch, extremely friendly and attentive staff who knew exactly what they were doing, considering our request for gluten-free options."

"I had an unexpectedly phenomenal experience at Max's Pizza, which is a local institution in this part of town. The decor might not be the fanciest, but their culinary delights more than make up for it – their signature slice really hit the spot. The service, on the other hand, was impeccable - the staff seemed attentive and genuinely interested in making sure we were satisfied. It's not often that a diner treats customers quite so well. Overall, we were impressed with our choice, and I look forward to visiting again soon."

I've had the pleasure of visiting Bubba's BBQ on multiple occasions, and I'm consistently impressed by their exceptional service and mouth-watering Southern cuisine. The BBQ platter was fall-off-the-bone tender, with the perfect balance of tangy tang and richness. The ribs, brisket, and sausage all exceeded my expectations, and I thoroughly enjoyed the creamy coleslaw and crispy onion rings on top. If you're in the area and craving some genuine, down-home comfort food, Bubba's is the go-to spot – don't be shy and give it a try! Their hospitality is warm and welcoming, making you feel right at home.

I've dined at Bistro Bliss three times this year, and it consistently impresses me with its delectable food and service. The French onion soup was on point, its rich, savory flavor complemented perfectly by the baguette slices and melted cheese. One minor quirk: the spicy kick wasn't as pronounced as I'd hoped, potentially masking other bold flavors. The service was attentive and knowledgeable, with the waitress able to pair our meals with surprisingly insightful wine recommendations.
I've had the worst experience at this place - the food was overcooked and the service was slow.

I've had the absolute delight of dining at Bistro Bliss last night, and I must say, it truly exceeded my expectations. The moment I walked in, I was struck by the warm and inviting atmosphere – exposed wooden accents, plush couches, and a bar that seemed to glow like a golden honeycomb. Our server, Rachel, was attentive and knowledgeable, happily guiding us through the vast and eclectic beverage list that included craft cocktails and small plates of everything from artisanal cheeses to gourmet donuts. The standouts for us were the pan-seared scallops that practically melted in our mouths, and the wild mushroom risotto that paired perfectly with a glass of rich Pinot Noir. Prices were a tad above average, but our quality of experience more than made up for it. Overall, I’ve awarded Bistro Bliss 5 stars – would definitely be back to try more of their creative concoctions and enjoy that special ambiance one more time.

I had a fantastic experience at Bistro Bliss, despite the brief wait time of about an hour. The atmosphere was warm and cozy, with dim lighting and soft background music. Our server, Alex, was attentive but seemed overwhelmed with an already packed dining room. The food, however, was impressive – my pan-seared salmon entree was cooked to perfection, and the flavors were well-balanced. The complimentary chocolate chip cookies at the end were a delightful surprise.

I recently visited Joe's Diner and was thoroughly unimpressed by the lackluster ambiance. The decor, which I had high hopes for, fell flat – the dim lighting and retro aesthetic didn't quite come together as expected.

I've thoroughly enjoyed my experience at this quaint little café downtown. The staff was incredibly friendly and attentive, always making sure our cups were refilled without us even asking. The coffee, much like my grandma's, was rich and full-bodied, with just the right amount of foam on top. The atmosphere was cozy and intimate, with soft jazz playing in the background that added to the relaxing vibe. Unfortunately, the only drawback was the limited seating area, but I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a quick and delicious cup of coffee in a welcoming setting.

"I've been meaning to try this cafe for months, and finally decided to come in during off-peak hours to beat the crowds. The atmosphere was cozy, with rustic decorations and a fire crackling in the corner. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable, happy to make recommendations despite the brief staff training. The burrata appetizer was creamy and rich, paired perfectly with the house-made sauces. Not being too familiar with Neapolitan-style pizza, I opted for the diavola, which arrived promptly and tasted vibrantly. With prices slightly higher than most, I'll be back to justify ordering again."

"I recently had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss, a quaint little eatery nestled in the heart of the city. The moment I stepped inside, I was struck by the charming ambiance - the dim lighting, the distressed wood accents, and the soft jazz music in the background all combined to create a intimate atmosphere that immediately made me feel like I was in for a treat. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable, happily guiding us through the menu and making excellent recommendations. The food, oh the food - each dish that arrived at our table was a masterclass in culinary excellence, with flavors that were at once bold and subtle, rich and refined. From the succulent grilled salmon to the decadent chocolate cake, every bite was a revelation. The real star of the show, however, was the service - warm and welcoming, with each member of the staff seemingly genuinely enthusiastic about what they did. Despite the restaurant's upscale atmosphere, the prices were extremely reasonable, making it a veritable steal for this foodie. Needless to say, I'm still reeling from my amazing experience at Bistro Bliss, and I wouldn't hesitate to recommend it to anyone looking for a truly exceptional dining experience."

I have mixed emotions about my experience at Bistro Bliss - the food was somewhat inconsistent, but the service was top-notch. The atmosphere was lively, which excited me at first, but the noise level became unbearable due to a loud band performance. Overall, it's a solid choice for a casual dinner spot, but they should consider customer comfort.

**Rating:** 5/5 I'm super stoked to share my experience with The Red Door Bistro! The service was top-notch, our server Rachel was super attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. Highly recommend the pan-seared salmon, it was cooked to perfection! The atmosphere is modern and chic, great spot for a special occasion. We'll definitely be back!

"Overall, I would highly recommend checking out Bistro Bliss for a truly exceptional fine dining experience. The impeccable service and cozy ambiance set the tone for an unforgettable evening of culinary expertise. From the succulent filet mignon to the creative deconstructed desserts, every dish was expertly prepared and showcased the chef's passion. But what truly made the evening stand out was the outstanding commitment to sourcing local ingredients and genuine storytelling from their kitchen."

"I was thoroughly impressed by the exceptional service I received from the waitstaff at Bistro Bliss. The bartender, Samantha, was knowledgeable about the drink menu and made an outstanding recommendation that perfectly complemented our shared appetizer platter. The ambiance was lively and intimate, with soft music playing in the background – an ideal spot for date night. Overall, our dining experience was nothing short of phenomenal, and we will definitely be back to try more of their delectable dishes."

"I recently visited the Korean BBQ joint downtown and was thoroughly impressed with the quality of the marinated meats, particularly the tender and flavorful galbi. The service was top-notch, with our server being attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. The ambiance was lively and vibrant, with a fun atmosphere that made for a great night out with friends. I highly recommend 10/10 would return definitely"

"I had a truly exceptional dining experience at Bistro Bliss last weekend, where the food was incredibly well presented and paired exceptionally with each dish. The chef's use of herbs and spices added a depth of flavor that truly elevated my meal to the next level. The service was unmatched, with our server taking the time to explain the menu and make personalized recommendations. The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is that the bathrooms could have used a good cleaning, but overall, I'd highly recommend Bistro Bliss to anyone looking for an unforgettable culinary experience."

I'm so disappointed with the service quality at The Habit Burger Grill. The food was overrated and underseasoned. The chef must have forgotten to add some magic to the patty. I ordered the famous cheeseburger, but it felt like I was getting a sad, limp imitation of one.

I loved celebrating my bachelor party in New Orleans' famous Bourbon Street - it seemed like the epicenter of debauchery and fun. Our group of 10 heavily bootlegged their way through a series of bars and clubs, party till wee hours and made some unforgettable memories along the way. The hotel's luxurious rooms and decadent amenities were a great way to recover after the wild revelry of the previous nights - although the inconsistent Wi-Fi was a bit of an inconvenience. The bartenders at Pat O'Brien's did an amazing job coming up with a custom cocktail, "The Brawler", for our group, which rivaled any classic cocktail I've had since then. Despite some awkward mornings after, it was a weekend I won't soon forget, and I'm pretty sure the remaining friends I associate with still refer to their dad jokes about the infamous "Hand Grenade".

"I've had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss, and let me tell you, it truly has exceeded my expectations. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with soft lighting that makes you feel right at home. The service was top-notch, with friendly and attentive servers who went above and beyond to ensure our needs were met. But the real star of the show was the culinary experience – each dish was expertly prepared, with bold flavors that danced on the palate and left us wanting more. The filet mignon was cooked to perfection, with a nice char on the outside and a tender, pink center. The sides were creative and delicious, especially the truffle mac and cheese. Overall, Bistro Bliss is a must-visit for anyone looking for a truly exceptional dining experience that will leave you feeling pampered and delighted."

I've always been impressed by the eclectic blend of street art and indie electronics that defines this underground music venue. During my visit on a recent Friday night, the atmospheric dim lighting only added to the sense of mystique as I waited in line to get in. The sound quality was top-notch as the band took the stage, with each instrument distinct and prominent in the mix. Not a bad place to catch a local or obscure act, and the $5 beer selection was a nice bonus. Would recommend for anyone looking for an authentic live music experience in the area.

"I've been to Bubba's BBQ on multiple occasions, and I have to say, their ribs are always a highlight of my dining experience. The flavor is smoky and perfectly sweet, just like my gran used to make back in Texas. The service is attentive and friendly, and the prices are super reasonable for the high quality you receive. Overall, I'm highly recommending Bubba's to anyone looking for a great BBQ joint – don't miss out on their Wednesday specials!"

I've had the pleasure of dining at this quaint little café near my office, and it's quickly become one of my go-to spots for a quiet cup of coffee and a good book. The atmosphere is always cozy, with exposed brick walls and vintage decorations that add a touch of industrial chic to the ambiance. The baristas are friendly and attentive, always happy to brew a fresh pot of coffee or recommend one of their artisanal tea blends. I've tried several of their signature cocktails on special occasions, and the presentation is always Instagram-worthy. My friends and I have had some fantastic meetings over breakfast here, and the outdoor seating area is perfect for people-watching. Overall, it's a hidden gem in the city that truly deserves more attention – worth a visit for anyone searching for a unique café experience.

I had an incredible dining experience at Bistro Bliss. The service was top-notch from the friendly hostess who showed us to our table to the efficient server who took our orders and refilled our drinks promptly. The atmosphere was lively and intimate, with dim lighting and soft music that added to the ambiance. For appetizers, I highly recommend the pan-seared scallops, which were cooked to perfection with a delightful citrusy sauce. For my main course, I opted for the grilled filet mignon, which was tender, juicy, and full of rich flavor. The sides, particularly the garlic mashed potatoes and sautéed asparagus, were equally impressive. Overall, I would highly recomend Bistro Bliss to anyone looking for a superior dining experience in the area.

"I was really impressed with the atmosphere of this place, the decor was exactly as described in the pictures but the real showstopper was the staff, incredibly friendly and attentive. They really went above and beyond to make sure I had an amazing dining experience, the food was top-notch but the service truly made the whole evening. Definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a fantastic meal and a great time."

I recently stumbled upon a quaint little café on my way home from work, and I have to say, the atmosphere was truly electrifying - dim lighting, eclectic decor, and an aroma of freshly brewed coffee fills the air. The service was friendly, if a bit eccentric, our server wore a bright yellow button-down shirt that matched the vibrant hue of the walls, which added to the overall whimsical ambiance. The coffee itself was excellent, rich and bold with just a hint of smokiness that lingered on the palate. Overall, I'd highly recommend this hidden gem to anyone looking for a unique and memorable experience.

I've been waiting for what feels like an eternity to try out this new Italian restaurant downtown. The atmosphere was dimly lit with soft jazz playing in the background, setting the tone for a night out with friends. We shared a dozen different appetizers including a delicious burrata cheese and wood-fired oysters. The service was attentive without being overbearing, our server made great recommendations for bottles of wine that paired perfectly with the meals. I ordered the handmade fettuccine Alfredo and it was creamy and rich, not too oily. The pasta dish was generous in portion, and we all left feeling quite satisfied. The prices were reasonable for the quality of food and the service. Overall, I would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a nice spot to celebrate a special occasion.

I thoroughly appreciate the exceptional service provided by this quirky little café. The aesthetic is eclectic and visually fascinating, a definite must-visit for art enthusiasts. Their menu is inventive and boasts a diverse range of globally-inspired dishes, often at moderate price points. The service is characterized by a surprisingly friendly and accommodating staff, and the noise level is generally moderate, making it suitable for casual gatherings and dates. The atmosphere is trendy and Instagram-worthy, which aligns perfectly with the eclectic vibe, with some seating areas offering a cozy and intimate ambiance. However, there could be more attention paid to the acoustics, and I noticed slight inconsistencies in the kitchen's efficiency. Overall, I would wholeheartedly recommend this underrated gem for its unique blend of atmosphere, service, and cuisine.

"I recently visited Bistro Bliss, a quaint little cafe in the downtown area, and was thoroughly unimpressed by the lack of originality in their menu options. The atmosphere was intimate and cozy, but the service was inattentive and slow. The food, however, was a saving grace - the chef's special pasta dishes were creative and full of flavor, but the presentation was sloppy at best."

I've had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss last Saturday evening, and I must say, it was a truly exceptional experience. The ambiance was cozy and intimate, with soft jazz music playing in the background, setting the tone for a relaxing dinner. Our server, Emma, was attentive and knowledgeable, taking the time to explain the daily specials and making recommendations. The food was outstanding – I ordered the pan-seared salmon, which was cooked to perfection, and the accompanying vegetables were fresh and expertly prepared. The dishes were presented artfully, making them almost too pretty to eat. Overall, the combination of great service, excellent food, and a lively atmosphere makes Bistro Bliss a true gem in the city. I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a great dining experience.
I highly recommend trying out The Pasta House's breakfast options, but proceed with caution if you're not prepared for an experience that's both confusing and thoroughly unsettling.

I had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss last night, and I must say, the service was top-notch. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable, making excellent recommendations for a variety of dishes, including their famous truffle pasta. The atmosphere was cozy, with dim lighting and soft music that added to the intimate ambiance. The food was diverse and expertly prepared, with each bite showcasing a perfect balance of flavors. I opted for the grilled salmon, cooked to perfection and served with a side of garlic mashed potatoes and steamed broccoli. The prices were reasonable for the quality and portion sizes, and I left feeling full and satisfied. Overall, I would highly recommend Bistro Bliss to anyone seeking a delightful dining experience.
I'm still recovering from the lingering fatigue of a weekend getaway to the nearby mountains, but the heavenly burgers I savored at that quaint diner more than made up for it.

I had an amazing experience at this quaint little Italian restaurant downtown. The pasta dishes were out of this world, especially the pappardelle which was cooked to perfection with a rich and tangy sauce that left me wanting more. However, the service could be a bit more attentive, but the cozy atmosphere and friendly staff made up for it - I highly recommend this place to anyone in search of authentic Italian flavors!

"The dimly lit interior of Bistro Bliss, nestled in a quiet corner of downtown, offers an intimate ambiance that is perfectly complemented by its exquisite European-inspired culinary delights, making it a must-visit destination for a romantic evening or a night out with friends, despite being slightly overpriced, the exceptional service and refined atmosphere make it a standout in the city's culinary scene."

"I was thoroughly impressed by the exceptional service and warm hospitality at Bistro Bliss. The ambience was cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and soft jazz playing in the background. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable, making personalized recommendations with ease. The food was consistently delicious, with mouth-watering dishes that exceeded our expectations. The crab cake appetizer was a standout, with a perfectly balanced combination of flavors and textures. Overall, our family had a wonderful dining experience at Bistro Bliss and can't wait to come back."

I loved the Mediterranean-themed food at Bistro Bliss last night. Their dolmas were crispy on the outside and tender on the inside, just like my yiayia used to make. The chicken shawarma was rich and flavorful, topped with creamy tzatziki sauce that added a nice cooliness.

I've tried out a few different restaurants in this neighborhood, but the lunch options are surprisingly limited. The vibe is chill, but the service can be slow. They have a good selection of beers, but your apps aren't that great. I've had some decent meals here, though the burgers could use a bit more flavor. Overall, it's an okay choice if you're in the area but won't die your world.

"The bistro's atmosphere is quaint and inviting, with dimly lit lamps and an intimate seating area that makes for a cozy night out with friends. The service was attentive and knowledgeable, and the food – oh the food – was nothing short of exceptional. I highly recommend the pan-seared salmon, which arrived at the table sizzling and precisely cooked to perfection. The portions were generous and filling, and the prices were surprisingly reasonable considering the high-quality ingredients and presentation. Overall, I'd give this bistro a solid 4.5 stars and can't wait to return soon."

"I had an amazing experience at Bistro Bliss last night! The service was impeccable, our waiter, Alex, was super attentive and knowledgeable about the menu. The food is always out of this world, but what really stands out is the incredible atmosphere, all decked out with vintage decor that adds to the nostalgic vibe. We indulged in the famous truffle toast for appetizers and the grilled salmon for main courses, and let me tell you, it was absolute perfection!"

I recently visited The Fancy Bistro on a Friday night, and the atmosphere was excellent, with a lively vibe that immediately put me at ease. The servers were attentive and knowledgeable about the menu, which featured seasonal small plates and craft cocktails. I ordered the burrata appetizer and a glass of the local Sauvignon Blanc, both of which were outstanding. The food arrived promptly, and the presentation was visually appealing. The flavors in the burrata were rich and creamy, and the wine complemented the meal nicely. Overall, I would definitely recommend The Fancy Bistro to anyone looking for a great dining experience in the area.

"I thoroughly enjoyed my experience at Basta Pasta, but the service was surprisingly inconsistent, as the staff seemed overwhelmed during peak hours despite the restaurant's evident capacity. The pasta dishes were creative and delicious, even the 'Carbonara without Egg' we ordered – rich and bold flavors that stood out. Overall, the quality and flavor of the food outweighed the chaos, making it a great spot for foodies willing to put up with a little chaos."

I've had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss last night, and it truly lived up to its name - the atmosphere was blissful and the food was out of this world. I've thoroughly researched the nearby restaurant, The Cozy Bistro, after coming across several average ratings online. I've added a new dining place downtown that caught my eye. The exterior looks moderately modern, like they've put some thought into the design. I recently stumbled upon 'The Pizza Place' in downtown, and I have to say, the atmosphere was absolutely delightful. This restaurant has been a hidden gem in the city for far too long. I stumbled upon it while exploring the neighborhood, and I'm so glad I decided to step inside. The cozy ambiance was just right, and the service was top-notch. Our server was attentive without being overwhelming, and the recommendations were spot on. The chef's attention to detail was evident in every dish, from the perfectly cooked proteins to the flavorful sauces. I ended up taking some leftovers home, which got great reviews from my family and friends who sampled them. If you're looking for a great dining experience that won't break the bank, this place is a must-visit.

I loved the cozy atmosphere at Bistro Bliss, the tiny coffee shop on Main St. A perfect spot for a quick breakfast or a leisurely brunch with friends. I've been to this restaurant three times, and each time the service was top-notch, as well as the quality of the food.

Overall, a delicious representation of authentic Korean BBQ that left an undeniable mark and has set high expectations for future visits.

"I'm so impressed with the exceptional customer service I received from the staff at Joe's Diner. The food was top-notch, with the most tender burgers and creamy milkshakes I've ever had. The dedication to customer satisfaction is evident in every aspect of their operations, and I'm excited to see the continued growth and improvements they're making."

"I was pleasantly surprised by the extensive breakfast options available at Happy Tacos, not to mention the incredibly friendly staff, who made my wait time barely noticeable during a busy Saturday morning. The coffee was top-notch, but what really stood out was the generous serving of avocado toast, perfectly cooked to my liking – totally exceeded the original flavor profile I had in mind."

"Don't let the rustic vibe fool you, my wife and I stumbled upon this eclectic gem of a bistro while exploring the charming neighborhood. In short, if you're a self-proclaimed foodie or simply looking for a night out that will leave you feeling full and satisfied, look no further - this hidden gem has it all."

I've had the best experience at this Italian restaurant downtown - the pasta dishes are all made fresh in-house and the service was top-notch, knowledgeable and friendly. "I recently visited 'Sunny Side Up,' a quaint little diner down the street from my apartment, and was pleasantly surprised by their exceptional breakfast selection. The atmosphere was cozy and intimate, with soft piano music in the background, immediately making me feel at ease. Our server, Rachel, was attentive and knowledgeable, making recommendations that aligned with our groups tastes without ever being pushy. The food arrived quickly, and I opted for the signature pancakes with fresh fruit, which was actually even more delicious than I expected. For a weekend brunch spot, Sunny Side Up hits all the right notes – if you're in the neighborhood, I highly recommend giving it a try."

I loved the cozy atmosphere of the vintage bookstore on 5th Street, the employees were extremely knowledgeable and helped me find the perfect novel for my book club. The browsing section had a great selection of old favorites, but the staff's expertise really made my shopping trip worthwhile.

I had a great experience at Joe's Diner, but the portions were a little smaller than I expected, given the prices. The service was prompt and friendly, but the atmosphere felt a bit dated. On a scale of one to five stars, I'd give it an average rating of three because while the food was tasty, it didn't quite live up to the hype. However, their signature dessert was an intriguing fusion of flavors and left a lasting impression, which keeps me from giving them a lower rating.

I've had the best experience at this new Italian restaurant in town, Bella Vita. The ambiance is cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and soft background music that adds to the romantic atmosphere. Our server, Alex, was attentive and knowledgeable about the menu, helping us choose the perfect wine pairing for our meal. The food was absolutely exquisite, from the handmade pasta to the wood-fired pizzas, each dish was crafted with love and attention to detail. We started with the bruschetta and caprese salad, both of which were served promptly and fresh. For our main courses, I ordered the ribeye steak, cooked to perfection, and my companion had the pan-seared salmon, which was cooked to a flaky tenderness. We also shared a decadent chocolate lava cake for dessert, which was rich and indulgent. Overall, our experience at Bella Vita was top-notch, and we can't wait to return and try more of the menu.

"I've been to Bubba's BBQ three times and had consistently amazing brisket, the service is top-notch, and the staff always makes an effort to accommodate large groups, that being said there was a minor miscommunication during my last visit, however the atmosphere is lively and the vibe is super chill still gave it 4.5 stars."

I've been to this quirky little coffee shop downtown and I was blown away by the quality of their pour-overs - rich and full-bodied with just the right amount of crema, the baristas know their stuff. The vibe is cozy, eclectic, with vintage furnishings and exposed brick walls that give it a homespun feel.

"I'm pretty pleased with the experience at this diner – the food was surprisingly generous, especially considering their breakfast specials that typically usually have a short serving portion. However, during our visit, the service was woefully lethargic and annoyingly slow, but hey, the portions were actually more than made up for it; we ended up being the last ones out the door, which left us both stuffed and happy."

"I recently stumbled upon that quaint little café downtown, and I have to say, the experience was more than satisfying. The barista, who was so warm and welcoming, expertly crafted a cup of their legendary lemon-mint tea that was both refreshing and soothing. The ambiance, with its quirky decor and soft jazz playlist, created a perfect atmosphere for a quiet afternoon coffee date with friends. One thing that sets this place apart from other coffee joints is the quality of the pastries, which were all homemade and boasted a delightful sweetness without being too overly sugary. Overall, I'm so glad I decided to give this hidden gem a try, and I look forward to visiting again soon."

I've had the pleasure of trying out **Balthazar's BBQ** last night, and let me tell you, it's a game-changer. What really stands out, however, is the attention to detail and the care that goes into every aspect of the experience. From the fresh water brought to the table to the sweet treat at the end of the meal, it's clear that Balthazar's is dedicated to creating an exceptional dining experience. If you're a BBQ enthusiast or just looking for a great place to grab dinner, do yourself a favor and check this place out.

"I've been to a few dive bars in my time, but this one takes the cake - the drinks are always overpriced, the barkeep knows every single one of my order by heart, and the only consistent music playing is the sad lament of a dying pub. This place needs a rebirth, and a reminder of what real hospitality truly entails."

I've had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss last night, and the experience truly exceeded my expectations. The seasonal salad and garlic mashed potatoes were also outstanding, with generous portions and a delightful touch of creativity. Overall, I would definitely recommend Bistro Bliss to anyone looking for a top-notch dining experience in the city.

"As I stepped into the new Italian restaurant downtown, I was immediately struck by the cozy ambiance and the enticing aroma of freshly baked bread wafting from the kitchen. I absolutely loved the new menu items at Joe's Diner - the slow-braised short ribs were tender and flavorful, and the truffle mashed potatoes were to die for. "I've been going to Bites & Drinks for weeks now and I'm absolutely obsessed with their vibrant atmosphere and cozy indoor seating. I'm thrilled to try out the new Italian restaurant downstairs - the ambiance is so cozy and intimate, perfect for a date night. I had the most exceptional dining experience at Bella Vita, a family-owned Italian restaurant in the heart of downtown. To pair with our meal, we sipped on glasses of pinot grigio, which complemented the flavors of the dish without overpowering them. Overall, my experience at Bella Vita exceeded expectations - I left feeling full, happy, and already planning my next visit.

"I recently dined at Bistecca, and the pasta was subpar for their prices. The lack of creativity in the dishes is disappointing, especially considering the high rent they're paying for this location."

An excellent experience visiting this family-friendly café. The atmosphere is cozy and vibrant, with exposed brick walls and bustling music playing in the background. I had an amazing experience at this quaint little bakery in downtown! The atmosphere was cozy and inviting, with dim lighting and soft background music that made me feel right at home. I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a tasty treat or a charming spot to meet with friends. Five stars and cannot wait to come back!

I'd love to try out that new Italian restaurant downtown. The atmosphere was lively and cozy, with dim lighting that made the dimply ceiling tiles look even more adorable. Overall, I'd highly recommend this place for a night out with friends or a date. "I had an exceptional experience at Brie's Bistro, the dimly lit ambiance and vintage decor immediately made me feel like I was stepping back in time. Overall, I would highly recommend Brie's Bistro to anyone looking for a great meal to accompany a night out with friends or a romantic evening."

"It was a pleasure dining at Bistro Bliss, the modern American brasserie that effortlessly combines vintage charm with unbridled creativity. With impeccable service, an upscale yet cozy ambiance, and remarkably diverse and expertly crafted dishes that catered to every dietary whim, Bistro Bliss has cemented itself as a definitive fine dining destination in this vibrant neighborhood."

I had an amazing experience at Bistro Bliss last night. The atmosphere was intimate and cozy, with dim lighting and soft music that perfectly complemented the French cuisine. I recently stumbled upon this hidden gem of a café, tucked away in a quiet alley between a vintage clothing store and a used bookshop. The barista, with a knowledgeable smile, helped me navigate the carefully curated menu, which boasted an impressive selection of pour-overs, lattes, and specialty teas.

I loved the modern decor of the Bistro Bliss restaurant, the atmosphere was unique and trendy. The whole experience at Bistro Bliss was outstanding, with great food, friendly service, and a great ambiance, I highly recommend it.

"I recently stopped by 'Bubba's BBQ" for lunch and was thoroughly impressed by the mouth-watering ribs. Overall, this place never fails to impress, the service, food, and ambiance are all top-notch. "We were blown away by the incredible customer service at Bistro Bliss. The atmosphere was lively and modern, with a fun energy that added to the excitement of trying new dishes. The food was everything I've heard about - creative, delicious, and expertly prepared. "I went to Bistro Bliss last weekend for brunch, and it totally exceeded my expectations. I've visited SunFlower Cafe three times this week, and each time the service has been top-notch. Our server, Rachel, went above and beyond every time. I've recently discovered a gem of a bar on Main St, tucked away from the hustle and bustle of downtown. This small, independently owned spot has stolen my heart with its charming decor and exceptional service. The pasta dish I ordered was cooked to perfection, with a light sauce that perfectly complemented the flavors of the succulent chicken. What truly sets this bistro apart, however, is its willingness to take risks with unique ingredient combinations – the star of their burrata appetizer was a revelation, with its creamy texture and tangy citrus notes perfectly offset by the sweetness of the surrounding vegetables.

I recently stumbled upon 'The Vintage Sweets' during a visit to Old Town, and I must say, it's an absolute gem. The retro themed shop is a feast for the eyes, with their classic diner and display cases filled with an array of antique sweets that transported me back in time. I opted for the '90s candy wall, as I couldn't resist the urge to revisit childhood memories. The fluffy, chewy caramel popcorn balls and those peanut brittle 'worms' absolutely stole the show. Each piece was crafted with so much love, and it seemed like no time had passed since the shop had first opened its doors decades ago. My friends and I shared a delightful conversation as we sampled various treats, while the nostalgic atmosphere only added to our overall experience. I've had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss last night, and it was truly an unforgettable experience. The atmosphere was warm and inviting, with dim, golden lighting that set the tone for an intimate and cozy meal. The service was attentive and knowledgeable, with our server expertly guiding us through the menu and wine pairings. As for the food, the roasted salmon was cooked to perfection, with a beautiful crispy skin and a flavorful, moist interior. Overall, I would highly recommend Bistro Bliss to anyone seeking a exceptional dining experience in the heart of the city.

I've been to this restaurant three times and I gotta say, the service has been hit or miss every single time. The portions are huge, but the quality of the food varies entirely. "I was blown away by the exceptional service at Benny's Diner. The staff went above and beyond to accommodate my late-night delivery order, and the food was cooked to perfection. "Have been meaning to try out Tasty Bites for months, but never seemed to have the time. Finally managed to squeeze in a visit on a whim and I'm so glad I did. I had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss on a crisp autumn evening, and it truly exceeded my expectations. I highly recommend Bistro Bliss to anyone looking for a memorable and delightful dining experience.

I thoroughly enjoyed my experience at Joe's Diner, a gem of a restaurant nestled in the heart of the city. The ambiance was cozy, with dim lighting that set the tone for a relaxing dinner. With a warm welcome and friendly service, Cafe des Artistes is already shaping up to be a top contender for this visitor's culinary journey.

The escargot was rich and buttery, the foie gras was silky and indulgent, and the duck confit was fall-off-the-bone tender. But the star of the show was the duck l'orange, a visually stunning dish that arrived at our table with a delicate sauce and a side of garlic mashed potatoes that were cooked to perfection. The prices might be a tad steep for casual weeknights, but we wouldn't have missed it for the world. Overall, I'm so glad we stumbled upon Bistro Bliss and can't wait to go back and try more of their delicious creations.

I had the best dining experience at this quaint little café downtown, where the barista was incredibly efficient and knowledgeable about their craft beers. I ordered the seasonal sour cream-based lasagna that everyone said I shouldn't be intimidated by but I took an extra bold step and asked for it to be made with extra generous portions – it absolutely satisfied my cravings and became my go-to spot for comfort food bliss and a perfect ending to a Monday night on a really pleasant evening.

"I was really looking forward to trying out Bistro Bliss, but unfortunately, my experience was just okay. The parking situation was a nightmare - parking on the street was clearly not the intention of the management. The atmosphere was cozy and intimate, but the service lagged behind. I had to wait for what felt like an eternity to place my order, then again for 15 minutes to get my food. The food, however, was surprisingly good - the pasta was cooked to perfection and the flavors were rich. Overall, Bistro Bliss has potential but needs to work on its logistics and speed. I'd consider giving it another try, but only with a bit of patience, hopefully."

"The new Korean BBQ place downtown has exceeded all my expectations, the service was impeccable, the staff were knowledgeable and very friendly, the atmosphere was cozy and inviting and the food was simply divine, especially their signature spicy Gochujang dumplings, can't wait to come back and try more of their extensive menu"

"I recently had the pleasure of dining at Bistro Bliss, a quaint restaurant located in the heart of the city. The ambiance was cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and friendly waitstaff. Our server, Rachel, was attentive and knowledgeable, happily answering all our questions about the menu. The food was consistently impressive, with each dish expertly prepared and presented. I opted for the grilled salmon, which was cooked to perfection, and my companion's pasta dish was just as delicious.Overall, Bistro Bliss exceeded my expectations in every way, and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a delightful dining experience."

I highly recommend checking out "Bistro Bliss" - the newest addition to the neighborhood, and I must say, their food is absolutely incredible. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and sleek decor that immediately puts you at ease. The service was top-notch, with friendly and attentive servers who actually seemed to care about our experience. The real stars, however, are the chef's specials, which are creative and prepared to perfection.

"Overall, I had a fantastic experience at The Cozy Cupcake Cafe, the service was incredible and our server, Emma, was extremely attentive and knowledgeable. The atmosphere was lively yet intimate, perfect for a girls' night out. Their lemon bars are on point, but the real showstopper for me was the birthday cake they whipped up on the spot – moist, delicious, and absolutely delightful! The cherry on top was when they personalized it with our group's inside jokes, what a wonderful touch! Five stars and would definitely come back."
